summary.intent: Audit all agnostic data-display components against the spec to close Epic #225, including splitting bloated per-category test files into per-component modules and verifying spec conformance, proptests, and coverage.
summary.outcome: All 11 data-display agnostic components audited; one real spec drift found and fixed (TagGroup disabled-group focus behavior); test files split into per-component modules; all gates passed (8,019 unit tests, 12 conformance checks, 7 ignored proptests, 95.90% line coverage); committed and PR opened closing Epic #225.

repo: The `data-display` category has 11 agnostic components: Avatar, AvatarGroup, Badge, GridList, Marquee, Meter, Progress, RatingGroup, Skeleton, Stat, Table, TagGroup.

Existing per-category test files (`data_display.rs`) were monolithic blobs; the established pattern for split tests is a directory-backed module (`data_display/mod.rs`) with one sibling file per component, matching the `input/` and `navigation/` test layout.
Badge, Skeleton, Avatar, and AvatarGroup were missing spec-conformance anatomy checks entirely before this audit.
The `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` command does not support a `--category` filter flag; it must be run across all categories.
`cargo llvm-cov` with `--lcov` mode does not emit branch records (`BRF`/`BRH`) for this workspace configuration, so only line coverage is reportable from lcov output.

The fast CI profile (`cargo xci --profile fast`) runs 10 sequential gates including fmt, clippy, unit (8,019 tests via nextest), integration, adapter, spec snippets, error-variant coverage, mutual exclusion, snapshot count, and native coverage instrumented rebuild.

code.finding: Disabled TagGroup was accepting `Event::Focus { item: Some(...) }` and recording `focused_key`, violating spec prose that disabled tags must be skipped and disabled groups prevent interaction. Fix: disabled-group Focus events now clear focus state and stay Idle rather than storing focused_key.
New directory-backed spec conformance module replacing the single data_display.rs file; now contains 12 anatomy checks across all data-display components.

New directory-backed proptest module; only stateful/complex components (Avatar, GridList, Marquee, Progress, RatingGroup, Table, TagGroup) have proptests since stateless components have no machine invariants.

Spec reference code is slightly ambiguous: says 'Allow focus/blur for AT even when disabled' but transitions to Idle without setting focused_key — correct behavior is accept focus/blur events but never store focused_key while group is disabled.

workflow: When converting a single test file to a directory-backed module, naming the inner test module the same as the component import causes Rust namespace conflicts; move component imports into each individual test file to avoid this.
Running `PROPTEST_CASES=32` when executing ignored proptests is an effective way to validate the split compiled and executes without waiting for full case generation.
The TDD cycle for spec-drift fixes: update the unit test to encode correct spec behavior first, confirm it fails on the current implementation, then apply the minimal machine fix.
Using `awk` to parse lcov files by path prefix (`src/data_display/`) gives per-component coverage breakdowns without relying on `--summary-only` which aggregates the whole crate.
The worktree was in detached HEAD state throughout the audit; a `git switch -c` branch was required before committing and pushing.
Parallel cargo invocations compete for the Cargo build lock and serialize in practice; issuing them together still reduces wall-clock time once the first build populates the cache.

friction: `cargo xtask spec info` triggered a fresh xtask compile in the worktree because it had not been built yet, blocking early audit discovery.
The `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets --category data-display` flag is not supported, requiring a full-category run instead of a scoped one.
`cargo llvm-cov --text` output was too large to use directly; required a second run with `--lcov` plus manual awk parsing to get per-component numbers.
Coverage run with `--lcov` mode did not emit branch records (`BRF`/`BRH`), making branch coverage unverifiable without a different instrumentation approach.
Multiple Cargo commands serialized behind the build lock when run in parallel, requiring repeated polling with `write_stdin` to collect output.
The `coverage-native` gate in `cargo xci --profile fast` requires a full instrumented rebuild, making it the dominant time cost of the pre-push gate.
open_items: Branch coverage for `src/data_display` is not captured by the current lcov invocation mode; a follow-up could configure branch instrumentation or use a different coverage report format.
Marquee has the lowest line coverage at 92.97%; specific uncovered branches were not identified in this audit and could be worth a targeted follow-up.

The TagGroup spec at line 207 is slightly ambiguous about AT focus behavior on disabled groups; the spec prose could be tightened to explicitly state that `focused_key` must remain `None` even when focus/blur events are accepted for AT.

Badge, Skeleton, Avatar, and AvatarGroup now have anatomy-only spec conformance tests; they have no proptests (correct for stateless components) but also no behavior-specific conformance tests beyond anatomy structure.
